paper_title: Stable Isotope Analysis of Fossil Samples from San Gerardo de Limoncito

methods.description: Process for extracting and preparing fossil tooth enamel samples for stable isotope analysis to determine the paleoenvironmental conditions.

steps.description: 20 mg of diagenetically unaltered tooth enamel was ground and sieved (125 μm mesh) to obtain a fine and uniform powder.
steps.searchable_summary: Grind and sieve tooth enamel to obtain fine powder

steps.description: 0.5 ml of hydrogen peroxide at 30% was added to eliminate the organic matter.
steps.searchable_summary: Add hydrogen peroxide to remove organic matter

steps.description: After two hours, the samples were centrifuged and the hydrogen peroxide was decanted and washed again three times with type I water (grade HPLC 18.2 MΩ).
steps.searchable_summary: Centrifuge and wash samples with type I water

steps.description: 5 ml of a buffer solution, Ca(CH3CO2)2 - CH3COOH 1.0 M, pH 4.75, was added and allowed to rest for nine hours.
steps.searchable_summary: Add buffer solution and rest for nine hours

steps.description: The buffer solution was decanted and the samples were washed again three times with type I water.
steps.searchable_summary: Decant buffer and wash samples with type I water

steps.description: Ethanol was added, and the solution was left for 20 hours in an oven at 90°C to eliminate any remaining water.
steps.searchable_summary: Add ethanol and dry in oven at 90°C

methods.description: Determination of isotope ratios using a mass spectrometer to analyze the prepared samples for δ18O and δ13C values.

steps.description: Isotope ratios were determined with a Finnigan MAT 253 mass spectrometer with a dual inlet system and auxiliary Gas Bench equipment.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine isotope ratios using mass spectrometer

steps.description: Results were reported as δ18O VPDB and δ13C VPDB, normalized using NBS-19, NBS-18, and LSVEC to the Vienna Pee Dee Belemnite (VPDB) scale.
steps.searchable_summary: Normalize isotope results to VPDB scale

methods.description: Analysis of the average values of carbon and oxygen isotopes to identify patterns and groupings among the samples.

steps.description: Average values were obtained for carbon and oxygen isotopes.
The relationship between δ13C and δ18O values showed three groups.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify groups based on δ13C and δ18O values
